framework.name: Australia’s AI Ethics Principles and Voluntary AI Safety Standard
framework.issuing_body: Department of Industry, Science and Resources (National AI Centre / Australian Government)
framework.effective_date: 2019-11-07 (AI Ethics Principles); 2024-09-05 / 2025-10-21 page update for Voluntary AI Safety Standard — voluntary
framework.scope: Australia’s AI Ethics Principles provide a voluntary ethical baseline for designing, developing, and implementing AI in ways that are safe, fair, transparent, accountable, and human-centred. The Voluntary AI Safety Standard is a voluntary implementation guide for industry that sets out practical guardrails for organisations using, developing, or supplying AI systems, especially where higher-risk applications are involved.
framework.applies_to: Australian industry and government use cases referenced by the principles; the voluntary standard is aimed at organisations using, developing, supplying, or procuring AI systems, including businesses of all sizes and sectors, especially higher-risk deployments.
framework.penalties: No legal penalties for the voluntary standard or AI Ethics Principles; compliance is encouraged through guidance and procurement/governance expectations rather than enforcement. The 2024 mandatory-guardrails proposal contemplated future enforcement, but no penalties attach to the voluntary framework itself.

control.statement: Establish organisational accountability for AI governance and assign clear responsibility for safe and responsible AI use across development, deployment, and monitoring.
Assess intended use, context, and potential harms before adopting or deploying an AI system, including whether the use case is high-risk.
Test AI systems before release and at key lifecycle stages to verify performance, safety, fairness, and reliability against defined acceptance criteria.
Monitor AI systems after deployment and maintain processes to detect drift, incidents, and emerging harms, with prompt remediation and re-testing where needed.
Maintain appropriate human oversight and intervention capability so a person can review, override, or halt AI outputs or decisions where necessary.
Provide transparency to affected users about when AI is used and, where appropriate, how outputs are generated or influenced.
Protect privacy and security by limiting unnecessary data collection, securing data and models, and managing sensitive or personal information lawfully.
Manage fairness and non-discrimination risks by checking for bias, unequal performance, and disparate impacts across affected groups.
Document decisions, limitations, and assurance evidence so AI system behavior, risks, and governance actions are traceable and contestable.
Engage relevant stakeholders and provide channels for feedback, complaints, and contestation when AI causes concern or adverse impact.
